owen-sound:affordability.scope: Owen Sound city census subdivision household affordability with Grey Bruce regional food context.

supporting.label: Local affordability assessment
supporting.reason: The indicator is consistent with this interpretation. The configured population-distribution evidence is pressured and unclear.
limitations: Regional Grey Bruce prevalence, not an Owen Sound city-CSD estimate.
2021 Census structural baseline using 2020 income; long-form estimates are subject to sampling and random rounding.
Excludes households not assessable under the official core-housing-need definition.

The four Census household categories cover most, but not all, city households and economic-family counts are not a perfect mutually exclusive household distribution. Medians hide within-type hardship.

supporting.label: Grey Bruce household food-insecurity prevalence
supporting.reason: The indicator is consistent with this interpretation. Grey Bruce household food-insecurity prevalence is 18.3% of the published reference population.
supporting.source: Grey Bruce Public Health, Monitoring Food Affordability in Grey Bruce 2024
supporting.calculation: Households experiencing food insecurity in the Grey Bruce health-unit geography, 2021 to 2023 combined
limitations: Regional Grey Bruce prevalence, not an Owen Sound city-CSD estimate.

supporting.label: Owen Sound households spending at least 30% of income on shelter
supporting.reason: The indicator is consistent with this interpretation. Owen Sound households spending at least 30% of income on shelter is 24.3% of the published reference population.
supporting.source: Statistics Canada, 2021 Census Profile, Owen Sound city CSD
supporting.calculation: Owner and tenant households with positive income spending 30% or more on shelter costs
limitations: 2021 Census structural baseline using 2020 income; long-form estimates are subject to sampling and random rounding.

supporting.label: Owen Sound households in core housing need
supporting.reason: The indicator is consistent with this interpretation. Owen Sound households in core housing need is 11.4% of the published reference population.
supporting.source: Statistics Canada, 2021 Census Profile, Owen Sound city CSD
supporting.calculation: Households below an adequacy, suitability, or affordability standard that could not afford acceptable local alternatives
limitations: Excludes households not assessable under the official core-housing-need definition.

supporting.label: One-person household
supporting.reason: The indicator is consistent with this interpretation. The One-person household median case, using the matched 2020 MBM total threshold, is classified pressured with income at 1.48 times its matched basic-needs threshold. For scale only, the newer regional food and rental references equal 52.1% of the older 2020 median income; that mixed-year ratio does not determine status.
supporting.source: Statistics Canada 2021 Census Profile and Tables 11-10-0104-01 / 11-10-0066-01
supporting.calculation: income / matched basic-needs cost; affordability context publishes food and tenure-specific housing components separately
limitations: Median city-CSD income; MBM Ontario 30,000-to-99,999 threshold based on Owen Sound's 32,712-person census agglomeration. Housing scenarios use city-wide tenure medians rather than household-specific costs.

supporting.label: Grey Bruce nutritious food basket, family of four
supporting.reason: The indicator is consistent with this interpretation. The measured family-of-four basket was about 1% more expensive than in 2023, adding current regional food-cost pressure.
supporting.source: Grey Bruce Public Health, Monitoring Food Affordability in Grey Bruce 2024
supporting.calculation: Monthly cost of the Ontario Nutritious Food Basket for a reference family of four
limitations: Grey Bruce regional basket, not an Owen Sound-only grocery survey; excludes restaurant food, special diets, and transportation.

mixed.label: Owen Sound-area one-bedroom rental reference
mixed.reason: The indicator provides partial or ambiguous evidence. It provides newer rental-cost context, but it cannot establish a direction against the Census baseline because coverage and building samples differ.
mixed.source: Grey Bruce Public Health citing CMHC Rental Market Survey
mixed.calculation: One-bedroom rent used in Grey Bruce Public Health affordability scenarios from CMHC rental-market data
limitations: Purpose-built buildings with at least three units; utilities may or may not be included and new-tenant rents may be higher.

mixed.label: Owen Sound-area three-bedroom rental reference
mixed.reason: The indicator provides partial or ambiguous evidence. It gives a newer family-rental reference while remaining non-comparable with the older city-wide Census shelter-cost measure.
mixed.source: Grey Bruce Public Health citing CMHC Rental Market Survey
mixed.calculation: Three-bedroom rent used in Grey Bruce Public Health family affordability scenarios from CMHC rental-market data
limitations: Small local rental samples can be suppressed or imprecise; new-tenant rents may be higher.

contradicting.label: Couple-only economic family
contradicting.reason: The indicator moves against this interpretation. The Couple-only economic family median case, using the matched 2020 MBM total threshold, is classified affordable with income at 2.34 times its matched basic-needs threshold. For scale only, the newer regional food and rental references equal 28.2% of the older 2020 median income; that mixed-year ratio does not determine status.
contradicting.source: Statistics Canada 2021 Census Profile and Tables 11-10-0104-01 / 11-10-0066-01
contradicting.calculation: income / matched basic-needs cost; affordability context publishes food and tenure-specific housing components separately
limitations: Economic-family income and household shelter-cost concepts are not perfectly identical populations.

contradicting.label: Couple-with-children economic family
contradicting.reason: The indicator moves against this interpretation. The Couple-with-children economic family median case, using the matched 2020 MBM total threshold, is classified affordable with income at 2.35 times its matched basic-needs threshold. For scale only, the newer regional food and rental references equal 30.1% of the older 2020 median income; that mixed-year ratio does not determine status.
contradicting.source: Statistics Canada 2021 Census Profile and Tables 11-10-0104-01 / 11-10-0066-01
contradicting.calculation: income / matched basic-needs cost; affordability context publishes food and tenure-specific housing components separately
limitations: Average family size was 3.9 and is matched to the four-person MBM threshold. City-wide tenure costs do not capture bedroom needs.

contradicting.label: One-parent economic family
contradicting.reason: The indicator moves against this interpretation. The One-parent economic family median case, using the matched 2020 MBM total threshold, is classified affordable with income at 1.51 times its matched basic-needs threshold. For scale only, the newer regional food and rental references equal 47.2% of the older 2020 median income; that mixed-year ratio does not determine status.
contradicting.source: Statistics Canada 2021 Census Profile and Tables 11-10-0104-01 / 11-10-0066-01
contradicting.calculation: income / matched basic-needs cost; affordability context publishes food and tenure-specific housing components separately
limitations: Average family size was 2.6 and is matched to the three-person MBM threshold. Family composition and actual tenure vary.

contextEvidence.label: Owen Sound-area one-bedroom rental reference
contextEvidence.definition: One-bedroom rent used in Grey Bruce Public Health affordability scenarios from CMHC rental-market data
contextEvidence.source: Grey Bruce Public Health citing CMHC Rental Market Survey
contextEvidence.reason: It provides newer rental-cost context, but it cannot establish a direction against the Census baseline because coverage and building samples differ.
contextEvidence.limitations: Purpose-built buildings with at least three units; utilities may or may not be included and new-tenant rents may be higher.

contextEvidence.label: Owen Sound-area three-bedroom rental reference
contextEvidence.definition: Three-bedroom rent used in Grey Bruce Public Health family affordability scenarios from CMHC rental-market data
contextEvidence.source: Grey Bruce Public Health citing CMHC Rental Market Survey
contextEvidence.reason: It gives a newer family-rental reference while remaining non-comparable with the older city-wide Census shelter-cost measure.
contextEvidence.limitations: Small local rental samples can be suppressed or imprecise; new-tenant rents may be higher.

matchedHouseholdCases.source: Statistics Canada 2021 Census Profile and Tables 11-10-0104-01 / 11-10-0066-01
